items.title: CVE-2026-42897: Microsoft Exchange OWA XSS Zero-Day Actively Exploited (CVSS 8.1)
items.summary: A cross-site scripting flaw in Microsoft Exchange Server's Outlook Web Access (OWA) allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript in a victim's browser by sending a specially crafted email. Affects Exchange Server 2016, 2019, and Subscription Edition (Exchange Online is safe). CISA added it to the KEV catalog on May 15, 2026.
items.why: On-premises Exchange remains widespread in enterprise and government environments. No interaction beyond opening a malicious email in OWA is required to trigger the XSS, making phishing-to-session-hijack chains trivially weaponizable. Patch via Exchange Emergency Mitigation Service immediately.

items.title: CVE-2026-31431 'Copy Fail': Linux Kernel Root Privilege Escalation Added to CISA KEV
items.summary: A logic bug in the Linux kernel's algif_aead (AF_ALG) cryptographic socket interface lets any unprivileged local user gain root with a 732-byte Python exploit — no race condition, no version-specific offsets, no forensic residue. Works reliably across Ubuntu, Amazon Linux 2023, RHEL, and SUSE on kernels from 2017 onward. CVSS 7.8, CISA KEV added May 1, 2026.
items.why: The exploit is universal across major distros and cloud VM images, and the public PoC is trivially deployable. Any attacker who reaches a shell (via web app RCE, misconfigured container escape, or initial access) immediately has root. Critical for cloud workloads and shared hosting environments.
items.source: CISA / The Hacker News / Microsoft Security Blog

items.title: Megalodon: TeamPCP Poisons 5,561 GitHub Repos with Malicious CI/CD Workflows in 6 Hours
items.summary: Threat group TeamPCP pushed 5,718 malicious commits across 5,561 public GitHub repositories on May 18, 2026, injecting GitHub Actions workflows with base64-encoded payloads that exfiltrate CI secrets, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and OIDC tokens to a C2 at 216.126.225.129:8443. Over 33% of affected repo owners had credentials already stolen by infostealers. Two payload variants: SysDiag (mass backdoor) and Optimize-Build (dormant, API-triggered).
items.why: This is one of the largest single-wave GitHub supply chain attacks on record. Downstream consumers of affected repos may be silently building attacker-controlled code. Audit CI/CD workflow changes from May 18 UTC and rotate all secrets immediately if your repo was among the 5,561 affected.
items.source: The Hacker News / StepSecurity / Hudson Rock

items.title: China-Linked Webworm APT Deploys EchoCreep & GraphWorm Backdoors via Discord and MS Graph API
items.summary: Chinese APT group Webworm is targeting European government agencies with two new custom backdoors. EchoCreep uses Discord channels for C2 (file upload/download, remote command execution via cmd.exe) and has sent 433 C2 messages to 50+ unique targets since March 2024. GraphWorm uses Microsoft Graph API and OneDrive endpoints exclusively for C2, creating per-victim directories for staged file exfiltration — blending malicious traffic into legitimate Microsoft cloud activity.
items.why: Abusing legitimate SaaS platforms (Discord, OneDrive) for C2 renders traditional network-layer detection nearly blind. Security teams relying on blocklists or anomaly detection on known-bad IPs will miss this entirely. Behavioral detections on process lineage and unusual Graph API call volumes are required.
items.source: The Hacker News / ESET Research / Dark Reading

items.title: Mandiant M-Trends 2026: Attackers Hand Off Network Access in 22 Seconds, Dwell Time Rises to 14 Days
items.summary: Based on 500,000+ hours of frontline IR in 2025, Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 reveals the median time for attackers to hand off compromised access to a secondary threat group has collapsed from 8+ hours (2022) to just 22 seconds. Global median dwell time rose to 14 days, driven by espionage campaigns (122-day median for North Korean IT worker ops). Exploits remain the #1 initial access vector (32%) for the sixth consecutive year; voice phishing jumped to #2 at 11%.
items.why: The 22-second handoff stat fundamentally breaks the assumption that catching initial access within minutes is sufficient. Defender response windows must now be measured in seconds, not hours. The 'recovery denial' ransomware trend — deliberately targeting backups — also underscores that IR readiness and offline backup verification are now table stakes.
